label: Iran Killed
source: Al Jazeera / HRANA / Hengaw / Iran Health Ministry
contestNote: Iran Health Ministry (official floor): 1,500+ killed, 19,000+ injured. HRANA NGO: 2,900+ incl. 185+ children. Hengaw: 4,300+ total in first 10 days. Iran UN ambassador: ~10,000 civilian sites hit. Pentagon denies targeting civilian infrastructure. 3.2M Iranians displaced per UNHCR. Internet blackout (~1% connectivity / 310+ hrs) severely limits verification. Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly wounded. Day 22: Anti-war protests suppressed in Tehran, Isfahan, Tabriz — at least 14 arrests on Nowruz.

label: US Killed
deltaNote: 13 KIA confirmed: 6 Army Reserve killed in Kuwait drone strike (Feb 28), Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington (died Mar 8 from wounds), 6 KC-135 crew (names released by Pentagon — crash Mar 13 western Iraq, cause under investigation). ~140 wounded total. USS Gerald Ford fire (Mar 18) — no sailor deaths confirmed. Joe Kent (NCTC) resigned Day 19.

label: Israel Killed
source: Israeli health authorities / Haaretz / Times of Israel
deltaNote: 17+ killed in Israel total. Day 22 (67th wave): 6 civilians wounded near Rishon LeZion — no new Israeli fatalities confirmed. Mar 18: Elderly couple (70s) killed in Ramat Gan — first confirmed cluster munition fatalities. Savidor Central Station damaged. IRGC stated barrage was 'in revenge for killing of Larijani.' 2,000+ Israelis wounded total.

label: Gulf Region Killed
deltaNote: Mar 17: Abu Dhabi missile killed 1 Palestinian civilian. Dubai airport fuel tank drone strike (no direct casualties). UAE Shah Gas Field attacked Mar 18 (no direct casualties confirmed). Kuwait US Embassy closed (Rubio order). Saudi Arabia intercepted 37 drones. 16+ vessels struck in Persian Gulf total. Qatar intercepted Iranian missile; blasts heard in Doha Mar 17. Qatar LNG remains halted Day 22.

label: Lebanon Killed
source: Lebanese health authorities / Lebanon Information Ministry
deltaNote: 870+ killed including 110+ children and 68 women; 33 health workers killed. 2,150+ injured. 820,000+ displaced. Day 22: IDF continued strikes on Bekaa Valley arms depots and Tyre-area positions. Separate Israel-Lebanon ceasefire track remains active (France mediating; Hezbollah has not responded). IDF ground forces pressing deeper into southern Lebanon.

label: Brent Crude
deltaNote: Brent fluctuating $100–112/bbl Day 23. IRGC threatened $200/barrel if Strait of Hormuz fully closed. 20M bbl/day backed up at Strait; only 5M finding alternative routes — largest energy disruption since 1970s. US Navy announced tanker escort operations when militarily possible. Qatar LNG production remains fully offline. Asian LNG spot prices surged 18% overnight. European TTF gas futures jumped 22%. FAO emergency food price alert active. IEA SPR release cushioning oil market.

label: Flights Cancelled
deltaNote: ~500+ tankers stranded in Persian Gulf. Qatar LNG offline (Day 2) — global LNG and fertilizer supply chains disrupted. Hormuz full blockade threatened: IRGC vows no oil will pass. 20M bbl/day backed up; 5M diverted to alternate routes. UN FAO emergency alert on food price spike. US LNG export authorizations accelerated. European natural gas TTF +22% Day 22. Switzerland halted US arms export authorizations and closed airspace to US military flights linked to Iran campaign — citing neutrality.

label: Countries Affected
source: Al Jazeera / Reuters / France 24 / CNN / NewsNation
deltaNote: Iran, Israel, Lebanon, UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Oman, Iraq, Jordan, Azerbaijan, Turkey, Cyprus, France, Switzerland, Pakistan. Day 23: Oman, Qatar, and China positioned as potential mediators for back-channel talks. FM Araghchi rejects all ceasefire negotiations. IRGC threatens full Hormuz closure; 20M bbl/day backed up. CENTCOM claims air superiority over western Iran and Tehran. ~80% of Iran's strike capacity against Israel eliminated.
